content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Mr. David Bauer 
2150 River Plaza Dr., Suite 150 
Sacramento, CA 95833 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 1-16-099 
Dear Mr. Bauer: 
May 25, 2016 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Because you seek general guidance, we are providing informal 
assistance, rather than formal advice. Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the 
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 
18329(c)(3).) 
QUESTIONS 
l. Do Facebook ads created in Facebook's "Ad Manager" fall under the "500 characters or 
less" description for ads oflimited size in Regulation 18450.4, permitting the ad to use rollover or 
hover-over display, click-through or hyperlink to display the required full disclaimer? 
2. May the campaign fulfill the disclaimer requirement by providing a click-thru URL to a 
web page or informational Facebook page with full disclaimer information? 
CONCLUSIONS 
1. and 2. As currently structured, Facebook ads created in Facebook's "Ad Manager" fall 
under the ads of limited size, including those of "500 characters or less," described in Regulation 
18450.4(b )(3)(G)( 1 ). As stated in that regulation, campaigns may fulfill the disclaimer requirement 
by provided a click-thru URL to a web page, informational Facebook page with full disclaimer 
information, or other technological means that provide the user with disclosure information. We 
also note that other Facebook ad programs do not limit the number of characters. Thus, this 
conclusion is limited to the ads posted through the Fa(:ebook Ad Manager. 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 
--=--<......_ \ 


FACTS 
File No. 1-16-099 
Page No. 2 
You have a general question about independent expenditure advertising for candidates by 
general purpose committees. You ask about the Act's campaign disclaimer requirements as applied 
to Facebook advertisements created in Facebook's "Ad Manager." The program only allows 25 
characters in the title and 477 characters in the text. Moreover, Facebook ad manager also 
automatically resizes these ads for display on mobile devices and for their Instagram platform, 
where there is even less copy displayed. The program may have the ads "follow" users when they 
leave Facebook, and re-size the ads yet again. 
On May 13, 2016 you added the following facts: 
• You provided samples, including a mobile iteration to illustrate how the ad gets resized for 
vertical display. You also noted that an ad will often require the viewer to click on "more" 
to read the balance of a copy block, so you would never know if the disclaimer is going to 
show without the viewer scrolling or clicking on the ad. 
• Your proposed solution would be to (1) require that the name of the sponsoring organization 
appear in the ad, visible whether or not the viewer scrolls or clicks, but that the balance of 
the disclaimer, "not authorized by a candidate for this office or a committee controlled by a 
candidate for this office" plus the committee address would be immediately available to 
anyone who clicks on the ad; (2) if the ad does NOT click thru to a contact and info page, 
then the full disclaimer must appear on the ad itself, visible to all viewers, whether they 
click or scroll on the add or not; and (3) if the ad is a clickable video, it should conform to 
the normal disclaimer rules that apply to video. 
ANALYSIS 
The required disclaimers for independent expenditure advertisements on candidates by 
general purpose committees are contained in Sections 84501 et seq., Section 84506.5, and 
Regulations 18450. l et seq.2 
Regulation 18450.4 states: 
"(a) The disclosure requirements of Sections 84503 and 84506(a)(2) 
[regarding top two $50,000 donors] shall not apply to general purpose 
committees, as defined by Section 82027.5. 
"(b) Where a 'disclosure statement' or 'disclosure' is required for an 
advertisement under Sections 84503, 84504, 84506, or 84506.5, the following 
shall apply to the committee that authorized and paid for the advertisement: 
1 The disclaimer requirements for such ads are summarized in General Purpose Committee Manual 4, Chapter 
9, and FPPC Political Advertising Disclaimers Chart No. 2, for "Independent Expenditure Ads on Candidates." 


File No. I-16-099 
Page No. 3 
"(1) Disclosures shall include 'paid for by' in the same manner as, and 
immediately adjacent to and above, or immediately adjacent to and in front of, the 
required identification. 
*** 
"(3) The disclosures shall be presented in a clear and conspicuous manner 
to give the reader, observer or listen.er adequate notice of the identity of the 
person(s) or committee(s) that paid for the communication, as specified below. 
*** 
"(G) Electronic Media: The disclosure statement on electronic media 
advertisements must be presented in a clear and conspicuous manner. A 
disclaimer is not clear and conspicuous if it is difficult to read or hear, or if the 
placement is easily overlooked. An electronic media disclosure statement is 
considered clear and conspicuous if it meets the following, as applicable to that 
advertisement: 
"1. Text or Graphic Electronic Media Advertisement: The disclosure 
information in a text or graphic electronic media advertisement must appear in 
letters at least as large as the majority of the text in the advertisement, or 
alternatively, if the advertisement is limited in size ( e.g. a micro bar, a button ad, a 
paid text advertisement that is limited to 500 characters or less in length, or a 
small paid graphic or picture link) the disclosure is displayed via rollover display, 
link to a webpage with disclosure information, or other technological means that 
provide the user with disclosure information. In addition, the disclosure 
information must be visible for a period of at least four seconds and appear with a 
reasonable degree of color contrast between the background and text of the 
statement as to be legible. 
• •• 
"4. Electronic Media Advertisement Disclosure - Exception: In electronic 
media advertisements whose size, space, or character limit constraints (i.e., SMS 
text message) render it impracticable to include the full disclosure information 
specified in Sections 84503, 84504, 84506, 84506.5, or subsection (b)(3)(G)(i) of 
this regulation, the candidate or committee sending the mass mailing may provide 
abbreviated advertisement disclosure containing at least the committee's FPPC 
number (i.e., "FPPC # 18-5734"} and when technologically possible a link to the 
webpage on the Secretary of State's website displaying the committee's campaign 
finance information, if applicable." 
In addition, the following "not authorized by'' disclaimer is required in independent 
expenditure advertisements on candidates: 


File No. 1-16-099 
Page No. 4 
"(a) An advertisement supporting or opposing a candidate that is paid for by an 
independent expenditure must include the following statement: This advertisement 
was not authorized or paid for by a candidate for this office or a committee 
controlled by a candidate for this office." (Section 84506.5.) 
Your question concerns Facebook "Ad Manager" advertising that allows 25 characters in 
the title and 477 characters in the text. These are electronic media advertisements that are limited in 
size, as described in Regulation i 8450.4(b)(3)(G)(l).3 Under such circumstances, the campaign 
may satisfy the disclaimer requirement by providing a rollover display, click-thru URL to a web 
page, infonnational Facebook page with full disclaimer information, or other technological means 
that provide the user with full disclaimer information. Your proposed solution where the name of 
the committee/sponsoring organization appears in the ad and the rest of the full required disclaimer 
infonnation is provided through a rollover display or click through to a contact and information 
page would satisfy the disclaimer requirements. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (9 I 6) 322-5660. 
JWW:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
era] Counsel 
1stant General Counsel 
Legal Division 
3 They do not fall within the exception in Regulation 18450.4(b)(3)(G)(4) for electronic media advertisements 
such as text messages, where it is impracticable to include the full disclosure information and abbreviated advertisement 
disclosure may be used such as the FPPC committee ID number.
